2604.08275.title: Floating or Suggesting Ideas? A Large-Scale Contrastive Analysis of Metaphorical and Literal Verb-Object Constructions
2604.08275.paper_summary: Metaphor pervades everyday language, allowing speakers to express abstract concepts via concrete domains. While prior work has studied metaphors cognitively and psycholinguistically, large-scale comparisons with literal language remain limited, especially for near-synonymous expressions. We analyze 297 English verb-object pairs (e.g., float idea vs. suggest idea) in ~2M corpus sentences, examining their contextual usage. Using five NLP tools, we extract 2,293 cognitive and linguistic features capturing affective, lexical, syntactic, and discourse-level properties. We address: (i) whether features differ between metaphorical and literal contexts (cross-pair analysis), and (ii) whether individual VO pairs diverge internally (within-pair analysis). Cross-pair results show literal contexts have higher lexical frequency, cohesion, and structural regularity, while metaphorical contexts show greater affective load, imageability, lexical diversity, and constructional specificity. Within-pair analyses reveal substantial heterogeneity, with most pairs showing non-uniform effects. These results suggest no single, consistent distributional pattern that distinguishes metaphorical from literal usage. Instead, differences are largely construction-specific. Overall, large-scale data combined with diverse features provides a fine-grained understanding of metaphor-literal contrasts in VO usage.
2604.08275.paper_summary_zh: 隱喻充斥著日常語言，使說話者能夠通過具體領域表達抽象概念。雖然先前的研究已從認知和心理語言學的角度研究隱喻，但與字面語言的大規模比較仍然有限，特別是對於近義表達。我們分析了297對英語動詞-賓語組合（例如，float idea 與 suggest idea）在約200萬句語料中的上下文使用情況。使用五種自然語言處理工具，我們提取了2,293個認知和語言特徵，捕捉情感、詞彙、句法和話語層面的特性。我們探討：（i）隱喻和字面上下文之間的特徵是否存在差異（跨對分析），以及（ii）個別動詞-賓語對內部是否存在分歧（內部對分析）。跨對結果顯示，字面上下文具有更高的詞彙頻率、凝聚力和結構規律性，而隱喻上下文則顯示出更大的情感負荷、可意象性、詞彙多樣性和建構特異性。內部對分析揭示了相當大的異質性，大多數對顯示出不均勻的效果。這些結果表明，沒有單一、一致的分佈模式能夠區分隱喻和字面用法。相反，差異在很大程度上是特定於建構的。總體而言，大規模數據結合多樣化特徵提供了對動詞-賓語用法中隱喻與字面對比的細緻理解。
2604.08275.author: Prisca Piccirilli et.al.
2604.08275.authors: Prisca Piccirilli, Alexander Fraser, Sabine Schulte im Walde
